steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is A, $w \left(14w+9\right)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Identify the greatest common factor (GCF) of 14w² and 9w: both terms share a factor of w.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Verify by distributing: w · 14w = 14w² and w · 9 = 9w. Sum is 14w² + 9w. ✓

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — $w \left(14w+9\right)$: Distributing gives 14w² + 9w, which matches the original expression exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — $9w \left(14w+1\right)$: Distributing gives 126w² + 9w, which does not equal 14w² + 9w. ✗
  • Choice C — $14{w}^{2} \left(9w+1\right)$: Distributing gives 126w³ + 14w², which changes both the degree and the coefficients. ✗
  • Choice D — $23 \left({w}^{2}+w\right)$: Distributing gives 23w² + 23w, which does not match; 23 is not a factor of 14w² + 9w. ✗
A is correct.
